title: pimpinella khorasanica
intro: Pimpinella khorasanica, also known as Persian cumin, is a plant species in the carrot family (Apiaceae) that is native to Iran. The plant contains compounds with medicinal properties, including antioxidants and flavonoids, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Consuming Pimpinella khorasanica has been associated with various health benefits, including reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and antioxidant activity. The seeds of the plant can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and capsules, to harness its medicinal properties.
